tasks: Initialized the gLabs Automator project repository with base structure and configuration.

Fixed all remaining UI issues across Layout, SignUp page, Welcome pages, and general layout inconsistencies.

weeks.summary: Kicked off the work window with two projects simultaneously. Built and deployed the gLabs Automator landing page in a single day (Sep 8-10), including Firebase Hosting, the full landing page, dark mode, footer, and privacy policy pages. Immediately pivoted to busy_books and built the entire landing page, mobile mockups, BookSlider, desktop mockups, summary audio section, and footer within three days. Key lesson: Tailwind CDN issues on production Firebase Hosting require explicit version pinning — the CDN script approach broke due to CSP headers on Firebase.

Deep work week on the busy_books inner app. Restructured the entire folder layout, built the Login, SignUp, and Onboarding pages, and started building the core dashboard components including the Explore page, Library page, and Settings. A significant amount of work happened in one day (Sep 18) — nearly every inner app page was created or scaffolded. Key architectural decision made: separating auth pages from the main app layout using a protected route wrapper.

Dual-track week: initialized the HabbitTracker_V2 project on Monday with a full component code quality pass, then returned to busy_books for the rest of the week. Built Firebase Auth login, registration, sign-in, and sign-out flows. Transitioned busy_books from direct Firestore calls to an Express backend API — added Axios as the HTTP client, created author and category routes/controllers, and integrated MUI icons into CategoryBadge. Worked through the weekend building the 'For You' personalised book recommendations feature. Key error learned: when migrating from Firestore SDK to a REST API, inconsistent field naming between the Firestore document and the API response caused many broken UI components — required a systematic audit of every data model.

The busiest week of the busy_books backend integration phase. Built the bookmarks, recently viewed, read books, 'For You' categories, and books-by-topics endpoints all in a single day (Sep 29). Spent Oct 1-3 migrating all frontend data fetching from manual useState/useEffect patterns to React Query (useQuery and useInfiniteQuery) — dramatically improving cache management, loading states, and stale data handling. Added loading skeletons throughout the app and moved Firebase config to environment variables for security. Key lesson: React Query's staleTime and cacheTime need careful tuning — setting them too low caused unnecessary API hammering, while setting them too high caused stale user-specific data like bookmarks to not refresh.

Vap Full Moon Poya holiday on Monday. Back to busy_books on Tuesday — built password change with Firebase reauthentication, profile image upload to Firebase Storage, and the AlertModal component. Built the Author Details page and paginated All Authors listing. Worked on the weekend implementing cursor-based pagination to replace offset pagination for popular and latest books — offset pagination was causing duplicate results when new books were added during a browsing session. Key win: cursor-based pagination with Firestore's `startAfter()` solved the duplicate display problem cleanly.

Completed key AI and search features in busy_books. Integrated AI-powered book recommendations during the onboarding survey — using Gemini API to generate a personalised reading list based on selected genres. Built the Continue Reading section and the Views Tracking feature (incrementing a counter every time a book detail page is opened). Added full-text search across both books and authors with view count increment on result click. Key lesson: The Gemini API onboarding recommendation call needed a fallback — when the model returned empty or malformed data, the entire onboarding flow broke. Added defensive parsing with a static fallback to 'popular books' when AI output failed.

Deepavali holiday on Monday. Resumed work on busy_books Tuesday-Friday — added user search history and built the bookmarks toggle. Enhanced user profile creation with additional fields during registration. Completed the Author Details page redesign and built the full Contact page with form validation, API submission, user data pre-fill, and a success modal. Improved Testimonials and Image Comparison sections with modern design. The app's core feature set was now largely complete.

Final days of the busy_books project. Implemented the last feature enhancement (Feature X) on Tuesday Oct 28, then began transitioning to planning the next project. Used this week to review the entire busy_books codebase, document deployment steps, and plan the architecture for ProductHub — a multi-tool content management platform for developers and content creators. Research phase: studied competing tools and identified key features to build first (blog writer, email generator, Reddit post planner).

Il Full Moon Poya holiday on Wednesday. Primarily a planning and architecture week for ProductHub. Designed the database schema, planned the routing structure, and began building the base component library (Button, Badge, Card, AppLayout). Key decisions made: use a custom design system over an off-the-shelf UI library initially, structure the app around projects where each project can have blog posts, email campaigns, video scripts, and Reddit posts. The Tailwind CSS theme was configured with custom colour variables to avoid magic hex values throughout the codebase.

Continued the ProductHub component building phase — pre-commit work on project creation modal, icon upload, sidebar navigation, blog writer, and email generator layouts. This was a heads-down prototyping week without committing, finalising the component structure before the first real commit. The routing strategy was finalised: projects are the top-level entity, and all content tools (blog, email, video scripts, Reddit) are scoped under a project context.

ProductHub first public commits landed (Nov 19). The initial commit included a working blog post creation modal, project modal descriptions, and updated README. Nov 21 added the full Dashboard, Email Generator, Project Overview, and Reddit pages. Key architectural note: removed all outdated implementation guides before committing — the pre-scaffolded guides were contradicting the actual implementation approach and would have confused anyone reading the codebase.

Rapid feature expansion in ProductHub across three consecutive days. Added lucide-react icons, AIChat with animations, BlogWriter/BlogWriterFinal navigation improvements, BlogStatsChart, ProjectGraph, VideoScriptForm with AI generation, and EmailGenerator with AI-powered subject line generation. Integrated framer-motion for transitions. Multiple rounds of styling consistency fixes — button shadows, colour variables, hover states. Key lesson: introducing framer-motion alongside Tailwind required careful layering — the motion components need to wrap (not replace) existing Tailwind-styled elements to avoid losing class-based styles.

Planning and integration week for ProductHub's Material UI phase. Worked through the challenge of integrating MUI into an existing Tailwind project without CSS conflicts. Key challenge: MUI injects global CSS via @emotion, which can override Tailwind's utility classes — resolved by using MUI's `sx` prop approach and scoping MUI components to specific sections (mainly the Reddit component) rather than trying to use it everywhere.

Productive dual-project week. Completed ProductHub's MUI integration, ScanWebsite page, and AdCampaign page (Dec 8-11). On Thursday Dec 11, simultaneously initialised the textToVideo project. By Friday Dec 12 the entire textToVideo UI component library was built in one day — Card, Input, Progress, Select, Slider, Switch, Textarea, plus theme context, HomePage, and the main TextToVideoConverter. On Sunday Dec 14 laid out sample prompts and reorganised the converter layout. Key insight: building the UI component library first before the feature pages paid off — no ad-hoc styling inconsistencies throughout the textToVideo UI.

Feature-dense week for textToVideo. Built file preview and parsing for .txt/.csv/.json uploads, Firebase setup with routing, bulk delete, PromptLibrary selection, sidebar navigation, tab navigation, batch delete, checkbox selection, and Settings component refactor — all in two days (Dec 15-17). Added videoGen_ext on Friday (Dec 19), initialising the Chrome extension version of the tool. Fixed the mobile tab view responsiveness issue on Sunday. Key error: the Firebase `rewrites` config in `firebase.json` was missing, meaning all SPA routes returned 404 on hard refresh — adding `{ source: '**', destination: '/index.html' }` resolved it.

Shorter week due to Christmas holiday (Dec 25). Built the video gallery view mode toggle and improved panel responsive layout. Used the remaining days for review, test, and planning before year-end. Studied Chrome Extension Manifest V3 APIs for the upcoming videoGen_ext features and planned the WearIt-extension architecture — a clothing detection and virtual try-on Chrome extension.

End-of-year sprint transitioning into January. Dec 29-30: Built key videoGen_ext features — aspect ratio selection, ZIP download, EditPromptModal, download path selector. Also deployed videoGen_ext and started WearIt-extension same day. Dec 31: Implemented Google Sign-In via Chrome Identity API and BottomTabBar navigation. Jan 1 was New Year's Day holiday. Jan 2: Fixed Content Security Policy issues and standardised formatting across WearIt components. Key lesson: Chrome Extension CSP blocks inline scripts and certain URI schemes by default — explicit blob: and data: source declarations in the manifest.json are required.

Deep work week on WearIt-extension and then Pinterest board downloader. Jan 5-7 built clothing detection content script logic (DOM traversal with vocabulary scoring), QuickView component, dashboard routes, storage change listeners, and the full project README. On Jan 7 also initialised and completed the ultimatewebscraper landing page with Navbar, Features, Roadmap, Testimonials, dark mode, and framer-motion animations. Jan 9: Built the entire Pinterest board image downloader extension in a single day — all download modes, progress bar, single pin button, CSV fix, and metadata export. Key error: the Pinterest board selector was inconsistent between user boards and 'other user' boards — needed separate injection logic for each board type.

Duruthu Full Moon Poya holiday on Wednesday. Started the Pinterest pin data analytics Chrome extension (Jan 13) — initialised the project, built real data extraction from the Pinterest DOM, and fixed video pin fetching. Used the Thursday-Friday window to test analytics overlay reliability with a MutationObserver and plan the Sort Filter feature. Key lesson on DOM scraping: Pinterest uses dynamically loaded lazy elements — a static DOMContentLoaded listener misses most pins; a MutationObserver watching the main feed container is the correct approach.

Feature expansion in the Pinterest analytics extension. Jan 21: Implemented dropdown direction option, export with ZIP, card size presets, SortPin improvements, and warm lead panel enhancements. Jan 22: Built media download with ZIP support, media type detection, and animated download UI. Key architectural decision: all analytics data was held in a content script - side state object that persisted across scroll events — this allowed sorting and filtering without re-scraping the DOM every time.

Wrapped up the Pinterest analytics extension on Monday (Jan 26) with comments metric, warm leads feature, in-page sorted results view, and a full notification toast system. Spent the rest of the week planning and building the gemini_watermark_remover Chrome extension — studied Canvas API for image conversion, background service worker architecture, and Gemini API prompt engineering for watermark removal. Jan 30 committed the working watermark remover with background service worker and PNG conversion.

Highly productive Gemini Image Site week. Feb 2: Built the entire Next.js application structure in one day — Gemini API integration, image generation UI, history page, subscription management, sidebar navigation, EditProfileModal, and the Navbar. Feb 3: Added favourites, image preview modal with keyboard nav, loading overlay, prompts page, parallel batch generation, IndexedDB session persistence, aspect ratio support, and selection + bulk download. Independence Day holiday on Feb 4. Feb 5: Implemented API key rotation to handle Gemini API rate limits across multiple keys. Key error: the initial single Gemini API key approach hit quota limits within hours of testing batch generation — the solution was a round-robin key rotation with a per-key failure tracker.

Multi-project week. Feb 9: Built the X Developer API explorer project — Node.js CORS proxy backend, frontend integration, and documentation for all X API use cases. Feb 10: Fixed HabbitTracker_V2 lagging issue (traced to expensive recalculations on every render) and initialised the reddit-api-development playground. Feb 11-12: Heavy HabbitTracker_V2 work — responsive sidebar, Switch component, ScrollToTop, Firebase Realtime DB caching, completion animations, and code formatting pass. Key performance lesson in HabbitTracker: wrapping expensive habit filtering computations inside useMemo prevented the lag, but the real root cause was that every `setState` call was triggering a full re-render of the entire habit list. Memoising the filtered list reduced render time by ~80%.

HabbitTracker_V2 dark/light mode overhaul week. Feb 16: Added backend caching, dark and light mode to all components, fixed animation issues, and fixed VPS build errors. Feb 17: Renamed the project to Habbit Flow, added global habit view mode toggle, drag-and-drop reordering with auto-scroll, and fixed real-time habit list updates after adding a new habit. Key challenge: drag-and-drop reorder didn't persist to Firebase on fast drag operations — the local state was updating correctly but the debounced Firebase write was getting cancelled. Fixed by using a ref to always write the latest order rather than relying on stale closure state.

Analytics and responsive polish week for Habbit Flow. Feb 23: Full layout alignment pass across Dashboard and HabitCard on all screen sizes. Feb 24: Built habit analytics export, best weekly entry calculation, note editing/deletion in HabitDetailModal, and floating completion button refactor. Feb 25: Fixed analytics page UI, added image loading states to Login/SignUp, and shipped the onboarding process. Feb 26: Minor cleanup — removed redundant colour indicators. Sunday Mar 1: Major code and UI overhaul — Settings page rebuild, colour scheme update, consistency pass across all pages. Key lesson: the Analytics page had a critical calculation error — the 'best week' was being computed by sorting the raw completion `updatedAt` timestamps without grouping by ISO week, which gave nonsensical results. Fixed by proper ISO week bucketing.

Archive feature and Gmail extension week. Mar 3: Shipped the Archive functionality for habits (archive/unarchive with UI integration) and built the initial gmail_exporter_ext Chrome extension landing page. Spent the mid-week days (Mar 4-6) planning and preparing the Welcome page enhancements — mobile menu, 'How It Works', and 'Benefits' sections. Also planned the final colour scheme update from #FE5D33 to #ff8c5a to better match the Habbit Flow brand across all components.

Final days of the recorded work window. Mar 9: Shipped the Welcome page enhancements (mobile menu, theme toggle, new sections), archive filtering in Dashboard, badge fix, and global colour scheme update. Mar 10: Final HabitCard theme-aware completion badge styling, Dashboard controls consolidation, and a comprehensive UI fix pass across all pages. The Habbit Flow project reached a visually polished and functionally complete state at the close of this diary window.
